headline: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule ends in Hungary election landslide
summary: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by 45-year-old ex-party insider Péter Magyar who convinced a majority of Hungarians to oust him. EU Commission President Ursula von der Leyen hailed it as 'a victory for fundamental freedoms.'

headline: US military set to begin blockade of Iranian ports and coastal areas
summary: The US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz is set to begin, with President Trump stating implementation will take time. Iran has rejected a ceasefire proposal, emphasizing the necessity of a permanent end to the war.

headline: Pope Leo XIV criticizes Trump, says he has 'no fear' after scathing criticism
summary: Pope Leo XIV criticized violations of international law by 'neocolonial' world powers in a forceful speech during his Africa tour, hours after President Trump accused him of being 'weak on crime and terrible for foreign policy.'

headline: Global air travel severely disrupted after Iran war forces closure of major Middle Eastern hubs
summary: Many people are unable to fly as planned to destinations after the Iran war forced the closure of major Middle Eastern hubs, including Dubai, Doha and Abu Dhabi.

headline: Nigerian airstrike at village market kills more than 200 people
summary: A Nigerian airstrike at a village market in which more than 200 people are feared dead is the latest incident in which the military has hit civilian targets while pursuing Islamist militants or armed groups.

headline: Ghanaian footballer killed after armed attackers open fire at team bus
summary: The team was returning from a match when they were attacked by 'masked men wielding guns', officials say.

headline: Peru election extends into second day after ballot delivery fiasco
summary: Technical and logistical problems mean Peruvians choosing a new president and members of Congress continue to vote on Monday.

headline: Norway marks 15 years since far-right attacks with return of ministries to redeveloped area
summary: Fifteen years after far-right fanatic Anders Behring Breivik detonated a bomb damaging much of Norway's government quarters, the prime minister and his government celebrated the return of several ministries.

headline: UK Prime Minister Starmer calls for action on social media 'mindless scrolling'
summary: Social media platforms such as Instagram and TikTok need to take action to stop young users wasting hours mindlessly scrolling never-ending videos, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said.

headline: Britney Spears enters substance abuse treatment facility after DUI arrest
summary: The singer voluntarily checked herself into a treatment facility, three weeks before her court date for driving under the influence.
